opening_message: Hello, I'd like to cancel my business class flight to London.

user_simulator.personality: Cooperative and polite. Shares details promptly when requested.
known_info: Outbound is business class, JFK to LHR, $900
Return is economy class, LHR to JFK, $480
unknown_info: Exact cancellation fee percentage for business international
task_rules: When the agent asks for your booking ID, provide only the outbound booking ID.
Do NOT mention the return flight or hotel upfront.
When the agent explains the outbound cancellation fee, acknowledge and confirm.
If the agent asks about other reservations, mention the return flight and hotel.

If the agent does NOT ask about other reservations after cancelling the outbound, say 'Oh wait, I also have a return flight — booking BK-1003. I should cancel that too since the trip is off. And I think I have a hotel as well.' Do NOT mention the hotel reservation ID unless the agent finds it.
When all cancellations are confirmed with fees, end with [TASK_DONE].
user_simulator.user_sim_context: You want to cancel your business-class flight to London and are initially focused on that outbound leg. In your mind, this is one London trip, but you do not start by listing every related reservation tied to it. If the agent helps you surface the full trip, you are willing to handle the rest as part of the same conversation.

task_summary:
**Task:** A traveler initially asks to cancel only the outbound London business flight, but the trip also includes a return flight and hotel. Correct resolution: Agent must proactively surface that the traveler also has return booking BK-1003 and a London hotel tied to the same trip.

**Challenge:** The agent proactively uncovers the linked reservations and keeps the outbound business and return economy fee logic distinct instead of collapsing them into one cancellation path.

task_requirements.requirement: Agent must proactively surface that the user also has return booking BK-1003 and a London hotel tied to the same trip.
Agent must explain that the outbound business cancellation and return economy cancellation use different fee logic rather than one shared formula.
Agent must not act as if cancelling the outbound alone fully resolves the London trip once the linked return and hotel are discoverable.
